\\n13: class FnToCLI:\\n14:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n15:     A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\\n16:     line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\\n17: \\n18:     This is _very_ basic; supports:\\n19:     * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\\n20:     * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\\n21:         * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\\n22:     * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\\n23:     * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\\n24:         * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\\n25:     * Type deduction of default values\\n26:     * Supports async functions automatically\\n27:     * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\\n28: \\n29:     Anything else will likely error :)\\n30: \\n31:     Example:\\n32:     if __name__ == '__main__':\\n33:         FnToCLI(my_func).run()\\n34:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n35: \\n36:     def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\\n37:         self.fn = fn\\n38:         arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\\n39:         annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\\n40:         defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n41:         num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\\n42:         default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\\n43:         defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\\n44:             arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\\n45:         }\\n46: \\n47:         docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\\n48:         arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\\n49:         self.parser = ArgumentParser(\\n50:             description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\\n51:             formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\\n52:         )\\n53:         self.args: Namespace | None = None\\n54:         for arg in arg_names:\\n55:             optional = arg in defaults\\n56:             cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\\n57: \\n58:             if arg in annotations:\\n59:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\\n60:             elif arg in defaults:\\n61:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\\n62:             else:\\n63:                 raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\\n64: \\n65:             # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\\n66: self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\\n71:             else:\\n72:                 self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\\n73: import asyncio\n2: from pathlib import Path\n3: import types\n4: import typing\n5: from argparse import (\n6:     ArgumentParser,\n7:     ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n8:     BooleanOptionalAction,\n9:     Namespace,\n10: )\n11: \n12: \n13: class FnToCLI:\n14:     \"\"\"\n15:     A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\n16:     line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\n17: \n18:     This is _very_ basic; supports:\n19:     * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\n20:     * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\n21:         * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\n22:     * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\n23:     * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\n24:         * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\n25:     * Type deduction of default values\n26:     * Supports async functions automatically\n27:     * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\n28: \n29:     Anything else will likely error :)\n30: \n31:     Example:\n32:     if __name__ == '__main__':\n33:         FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n34:     \"\"\"\n35: \n36:     def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\n37:         self.fn = fn\n38:         arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\n39:         annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\n40:         defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n41:         num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n42:         default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n43:         defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n44:             arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n45:         }\n46: \n47:         docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n48:         arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n49:         self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n50:             description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n51:             formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n52:         )\n53:         self.args: Namespace | None = None\n54:         for arg in arg_names:\n55:             optional = arg in defaults\n56:             cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n57: \n58:             if arg in annotations:\n59:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n60:             elif arg in defaults:\n61:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n62:             else:\n63:                 raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n64: \n65:             # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\n66:             arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\n67: \n68:             if optional:\n69:                 opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\n70:                 self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n71:             else:\n72:                 self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n73: \n74:     def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n75:         self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n76:         return self.args\n77: \n78:     def args_dict(self):\n79:         if not self.args:\n80:             self.parse_args()\n81: \n82:         return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}\n83: \n84:     def run(self):\n85:         args_dicts = self.args_dict()\n86:         if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):\n87:             return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))\n88:         else:\n89:             return self.fn(**args_dicts)\n90: \n91:     @staticmethod\n92:     def parse_docs(docs):\n93:         params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]\n94:         params = [p.strip() for p in params]\n95:         params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]\n96:         return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}\n97: \n98:     @staticmethod\n99:     def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\n100:         if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\n101:             return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\n102:                 next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\n103:             )\n104:         if typ == bool:\n105:             return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\n106: \n107:         simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\n108:         if typ in simple_types:\n109:             return {'type': typ}\n110: \n111:         if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n112:             subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n113:             if subtype in simple_types:\n114:                 return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n115: \n116:         if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n117:             return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\n118:         raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')\n119: \n120:     @staticmethod\n121:     def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:\n122:         return (\n123:             (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))\n124:             and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)\n125:             and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2\n126: When called without arguments, it should return None.\n\nLet me analyze the current implementation in `fn_to_cli.py`:\n\nLooking at line 99-118 (`type_to_argparse` method):\n```python\n@staticmethod\ndef type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\n    if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\n        return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\n            next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\n        )\n    if typ == bool:\n        return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\n\n    simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\n    if typ in simple_types:\n        return {'type': typ}\n\n    if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n        subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n        if subtype in simple_types:\n            return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n\n    if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n        return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\n    raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')\n```\n\nThe problem is:\n1. For `list[Path] | None`, when the optional wrapper is stripped, it recursively processes `list[Path]` and returns `{'nargs': '*', 'type': Path}`. However, when no arguments are provided, argparse with `nargs='*'` returns an empty list `[]` instead of `None`. This creates a `--files` argument that will return an empty list `[]` when not provided, rather than respecting the `None` default. The behavior of argparse with `nargs='*'` needs to be verified to understand how to properly handle optional list arguments. When `parse_args([])` is called without `--files`, the default should be `None`, and the function returns `None`. For the list test, `nums: list[int]` needs to accept multiple positional arguments converted to integers, so `['1', '2', '3']` becomes `[1, 2, 3]` and sums to 6. asyncio\nfrom pathlib import Path\nimport types\nimport typing\nfrom argparse import (\n    ArgumentParser,\n    ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n    BooleanOptionalAction,\n    Namespace,\n)\n\n\nclass FnToCLI:\n    \"\"\"\n    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\n    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\n\n    This is _very_ basic; supports:\n    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\n    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\n        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\n    * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\n    * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\n        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\n    * Type deduction of default values\n    * Supports async functions automatically\n    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\n\n    Anything else will likely error :)\n\n    Example:\n    if __name__ == '__main__':\n        FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\n        self.fn = fn\n        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\n        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\n        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n        }\n\n        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n        self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n        )\n        self.args: Namespace | None = None\n        for arg in arg_names:\n            optional = arg in defaults\n            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n\n            if arg in annotations:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg in defaults:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n\n            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\n            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\n\n            if optional:\n                opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\n                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n\n    def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n        return self.args\n\n    def args_dict(self):\n        if not self.args:\n            self.parse_args()\n\n        return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}\n\n    def run(self):\n        args_dicts = self.args_dict()\n        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):\n            return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))\n        else:\n            return self.fn(**args_dicts)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def parse_docs(docs):\n        params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]\n        params = [p.strip() for p in params]\n        params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]\n        return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\n        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\n            return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\n                next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\n            )\n        if typ == bool:\n            return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\n\n        simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\n        if typ in simple_types:\n            return {'type': typ}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n            subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n            if subtype in simple_types:\n                return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n            return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\n        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:\n        return (\n            (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))\n            and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)\n            and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2\n        )\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n\n    def fn(nums: list[int]):\n        print(sum(nums))\n\n    cli = FnToCLI(fn)\n    cli.run()\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":244,"cache_read_input_tokens":42953,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":244},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"21da0b07-0daf-4f7a-a187-2d367a466c8c","uuid":"61403b5f-0799-4a50-b16a-667e8c8b24e9"}

        subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n            if subtype in simple_types:\n                return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JVARPdNPjn37GArK5MA2ae","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: import asyncio\\n2: import types\\n3: import typing\\n4: from argparse import (\\n5:     ArgumentParser,\\n6:     ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\\n7:     BooleanOptionalAction,\\n8:     Namespace,\\n9: )\\n10: \\n11: \\n12: class FnToCLI:\\n13:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n14:     A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\\n15:     line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\\n16: \\n17:     This is _very_ basic; supports:\\n18:     * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\\n19:     * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\\n20:         * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\\n21:     * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\\n22:     * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\\n23:         * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\\n24:     * Type deduction of default values\\n25:     * Supports async functions automatically\\n26:     * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\\n27: \\n28:     Anything else will likely error :)\\n29: \\n30:     Example:\\n31:     if __name__ == '__main__':\\n32:         FnToCLI(my_func).run()\\n33:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n34: \\n35:     def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\\n36:         self.fn = fn\\n37:         arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\\n38:         annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\\n39:         defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n40:         num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\\n41:         default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\\n42:         defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\\n43:             arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\\n44:         }\\n45: \\n46:         docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\\n47:         arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\\n48:         self.parser = ArgumentParser(\\n49:             description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\\n50:             formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\\n51:         )\\n52:         self.args: Namespace | None = None\\n53:         for arg in arg_names:\\n54:             optional = arg in defaults\\n55:             cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\\n56: \\n57:             if arg in annotations:\\n58:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\\n59:             elif arg in defaults:\\n60:                 arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\\n61:             else:\\n62:                 raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\\n63: \\n64:             # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\\n65:             arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\\n66: \\n67:             if optional:\\n68:                 opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\\n69:                 self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\\n70:             else:\\n71:                 self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\\n72: \\n73:     def parse_args(self):\\n74:         self.args = Namespace,\n)\n\n\nclass FnToCLI:\n    \"\"\"\n    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\n    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\n\n    This is _very_ basic; supports:\n    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\n    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\n        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes format\n\n    Anything else will likely error :)\n\n    Example:\n    if __name__ == '__main__':\n        FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\n        self.fn = fn\n        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\n        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\n        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n            arg: optional = arg in defaults\n            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n\n            if arg in annotations:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\n                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n\n    def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\n        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\n            return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\n                next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\n            )\n        if typ == bool:\n            return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\n\n        simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\n        if typ in simple_types:\n            return {'type': typ}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n            subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n            if subtype in simple_types:\n                return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n            return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\n        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: asyncio\nimport types\nimport typing\nfrom argparse import (\n    ArgumentParser,\n    ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n    BooleanOptionalAction,\n    Namespace,\n)\n\n\nclass FnToCLI:\n    \"\"\"\n    A utility class which automatically infers and generates ArgParse command\n    line options from a function based on defaults/type annotations\n\n    This is _very_ basic; supports:\n    * Args of int, str types (same logic as default argparse)\n    * Args of bool type (Uses argparse BooleanOptionalAction)\n        * eg `do_blah=False` becomes `--do-blah, --no-do-blah`\n    * Args of typing.Optional (or anything with a default)\n    * Args of typing.Literal (uses argparse choices)\n        * eg `color: Literal['red, 'black']` becomes `--color red|black` (with docs)\n    * Type deduction of default values\n    * Supports async functions automatically\n    * Includes docstring if it's in `:param my_arg: Description of my arg` format\n\n    Anything else will likely error :)\n\n    Example:\n    if __name__ == '__main__':\n        FnToCLI(my_func).run()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fn: typing.Callable):\n        self.fn = fn\n        arg_names = fn.__code__.co_varnames[: fn.__code__.co_argcount]\n        annotations = typing.get_type_hints(fn)\n        defaults: list = fn.__defaults__ or []  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        num_required = len(arg_names) - len(defaults)\n        default_args = arg_names[num_required:]\n        defaults: dict = {  # type: ignore[no-redef]\n            arg: default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n        }\n\n        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n        self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n        )\n        self.args: Namespace | None = None\n        for arg in arg_names:\n            optional = arg in defaults\n            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n\n            if arg in annotations:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg in defaults:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n\n            # Help needs to always default for [arg, default] in zip(default_args, defaults)\n        }\n\n        docs = fn.__doc__ or ''\n        arg_docs = self.parse_docs(docs)\n        self.parser = ArgumentParser(\n            description=docs.split(':param', 1)[0],\n            formatter_class=ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,\n        )\n        self.args: Namespace | None = None\n        for arg in arg_names:\n            optional = arg in defaults\n            cli_name = arg.replace('_', '-')\n\n            if arg in annotations:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(annotations[arg])\n            elif arg in defaults:\n                arg_opts = self.type_to_argparse(type(defaults[arg]))  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(f'{arg} has no type information')\n\n            # Help needs to always be defined, or it won't show the default :/\n            arg_opts['help'] = arg_docs.get(arg) or '-'\n\n            if optional:\n                opt_name = f'--{cli_name}' if len(cli_name) > 1 else f'-{cli_name}'\n                self.parser.add_argument(opt_name, default=defaults[arg], **arg_opts)  # type: ignore[call-overload]\n            else:\n                self.parser.add_argument(cli_name, **arg_opts)\n\n    def parse_args(self, args: typing.Sequence[str] | None = None):\n        self.args = self.parser.parse_args(args)\n        return self.args\n\n    def args_dict(self):\n        if not self.args:\n            self.parse_args()\n\n        return {k.replace('-', '_'): v for k, v in self.args.__dict__.items()}\n\n    def run(self):\n        args_dicts = self.args_dict()\n        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(self.fn):\n            return asyncio.run(self.fn(**args_dicts))\n        else:\n            return self.fn(**args_dicts)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def parse_docs(docs):\n        params = docs.strip().split(':param ')[1:]\n        params = [p.strip() for p in params]\n        params = [p.split(':', 1) for p in params if p]\n        return {name: docs.strip() for [name, docs] in params}\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def type_to_argparse(typ: type) -> dict:\n        if FnToCLI.is_optional(typ):\n            return FnToCLI.type_to_argparse(\n                next(t for t in typing.get_args(typ) if not isinstance(t, type(None)))\n            )\n        if typ == bool:\n            return {'type': typ, 'action': BooleanOptionalAction}\n\n        simple_types = (int, str, float, Path)\n        if typ in simple_types:\n            return {'type': typ}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == list:\n            subtype = typing.get_args(typ)[0]\n            if subtype in simple_types:\n                return {'nargs': '*', 'type': subtype}\n\n        if typing.get_origin(typ) == typing.Literal:\n            return {'choices': typing.get_args(typ)}\n        raise ValueError(f'Unsupported type: {typ}')\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def is_optional(typ: type) -> bool:\n        return (\n            (typing.get_origin(typ) is typing.Union or isinstance(typ, types.UnionType))\n            and type(None) in typing.get_args(typ)\n            and len(typing.get_args(typ)) == 2\n        )\n","numLines":127,"startLine":1,"totalLines":127}}}
